# Transcrição: 11-Promote Impact to Clients

- **Fonte:** 11-Promote Impact to Clients
- **Duração:** 04:40
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:02]

As a new business owner, the third type of promotion that you need to constantly be doing is to your clients, your customers, and your vendors. This seems obvious, but most business owners are just horrible at promoting their business, especially when they're making operational changes, people changes, you know, other things that they're doing in the business, they don't promote why they're doing it. They don't promote the benefit of it, so the community as a whole doesn't understand it. This is both a way to put fears to rest and also gives direction to people about what you're doing and why you're doing it. Don't forget, you have the power and the platform to make your promotional content about your customers, your clients, vendors, your key employees, promote all the things in the business that you want everyone in the community to understand. If you lost a key employee, promote what you're looking for in new key employees. The natural assumption will be that employee that left didn't have those attributes. So when there's something that you don't like or that you have to address, the more you can promote the counter side of that, okay, the intention of what you want to move to, the better your community will understand you. So as a new owner of a business, for example, you may want to shoot an Instagram story with the owner that sold you the business so they can talk about why they picked you, why they had confidence in you, right? So promote everything you can to create the image, the message that you want. From that handoff, you can then get to know the vendors, learn any concerns you share. So for me, if I bought a business, I'd be like, hey, John, Johnny the cameraman, why'd you sell me your camera business? Oh, Brandon, because you're a good guy and I really like you and I know you're going to take care of my team. I would put that out in the universe and then people are like, hey, you bought Johnny's business? Yeah, listen to what Johnny said, the cameraman, look what he said. He said, this is the guy I trust, right here. I picked this guy. I like him, I trust him. He's going to do a great job. I know he's going to take really good care of you. And so Johnny is now promoting me. And so the more you can do those type of things, and same with your employees. What do you love about working here? Oh, I love what we do for our customers. I love what we do for our clients. I love going over and above and always, always delighting our customers because of all the great things that we do. That's how you promote to your customers. Promote to your customers, your vendors, your employees. Remember, businesses don't move people. People move businesses. Your customers, your clients and your vendors are all people. Treat them like people by giving them the respect of personalized communication and the time to understand their goals. Then lead the business to help them achieve those results and they'll help you move your business forward and create a remarkable, valued business system. That's just to let them know you're behind the wheel.

## [03:02 - 04:40]

You can also let them know how to find out what else is going on in your business. In real time, think about this. You should look at what kind of promotion the seller did in your business. My guess is there's no links to your website. There's no Instagram links. There's no funnels being run. There's no promotion probably of any kind. This is the stuff we teach here at Cardone Enterprises. We teach how to promote properly to create massive value. Now, don't ever worry about being overly promotional. Maybe I'm coming across right now as overly promotional, but see, I don't care because I'm teaching you how to promote. Like, I'll yell at you. Hey! What are you doing to promote your business that you just bought? Are you going home and telling your wife how hard it is and how the employees aren't doing their job? Are you going out and telling your friends at church on the golf course how upset you are because the owner that sold you the business sold you a piece of crap? What are you promoting? Because whatever you're promoting, you're going to get more of. So here's the point. If you're really scared of oversaturation your promotion, be more afraid of what you are actually promoting. Oversaturate all the remarkable reasons you bought this business, what you want to build, why you wanted to build it. Do your promotions right and you'll create so much noise, you'll actually differentiate yourself in the marketplace from your competition. That's when people will come work with you because they'll see the opportunity that makes you different than everyone else.
